children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established the National Windstorm Impact Reduction Program, the purpose of which is to achieve major measurable reductions in the losses of life and property from windstorms through a coordinated Federal effort, in cooperation with other levels of government, academia, and the private sector, aimed at improving the understanding of windstorms and their impacts and developing and encouraging the implementation of cost-effective mitigation measures to reduce those impacts.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that the Program includes the necessary components to promote the implementation of windstorm risk reduction measures by Federal, State, and local governments, national standards and model building code organizations, architects and engineers, and others with a role in planning and constructing buildings and lifelines;

children.label: B
children.text: support the development of performance-based engineering tools, and work with appropriate groups to promote the commercial application of such tools, including through wind-related model building codes, voluntary standards, and construction best practices;

children.label: C
children.text: request the assistance of Federal agencies other than the Program agencies, as necessary to assist in carrying out this chapter;

children.label: D
children.text: coordinate all Federal post-windstorm investigations to the extent practicable; and

children.label: E
children.text: when warranted by research or investigative findings, issue recommendations to assist in informing the development of model codes, and provide information to Congress on the use of such recommendations.

children.heading: National Institute of Standards and Technology
children.label: 2 National Institute of Standards and Technology
children.text: In addition to the lead agency responsibilities described under paragraph (1), the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall be responsible for carrying out research and development to improve model building codes, voluntary standards, and best practices for the design, construction, and retrofit of buildings, structures, and lifelines.

children.label: A
children.text: engineering and the atmospheric sciences to improve the understanding of the behavior of windstorms and their impact on buildings, structures, and lifelines; and

children.label: B
children.text: economic and social factors influencing windstorm risk reduction measures.

children.heading: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
children.label: 4 National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
children.text: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration shall support atmospheric sciences research to improve the understanding of the behavior of windstorms and their impact on buildings, structures, and lifelines.

children.label: i
children.text: the development of risk assessment tools and effective mitigation techniques;

children.label: ii
children.text: windstorm-related data collection and analysis;

children.label: iii
children.text: public outreach and information dissemination; and

children.label: iv
children.text: promotion of the adoption of windstorm preparedness and mitigation measures, including for households, businesses, and communities, consistent with the Agency’s all-hazards approach; and

children.label: B
children.text: work closely with national standards and model building code organizations, in conjunction with the National Institute of Standards and Technology, to promote the implementation of research results and promote better building practices within the building design and construction industry, including architects, engineers, contractors, builders, and inspectors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Program shall consist of three primary mitigation components: improved understanding of windstorms, windstorm impact assessment, and windstorm impact reduction. The components shall be implemented through activities such as data collection and analysis, risk assessment, outreach, technology transfer, and research and development. To the extent practicable, research activities authorized under this chapter shall be peer-reviewed, and the components shall be designed to be complementary to, and avoid duplication of, other public and private hazard reduction efforts.

children.heading: Understanding of windstorms
children.label: 2 Understanding of windstorms
children.text: Activities to enhance the understanding of windstorms shall include research to improve knowledge of and data collection on the impact of severe wind on buildings, structures, and infrastructure.

children.label: A
children.text: development of mechanisms for collecting and inventorying information on the performance of buildings, structures, and infrastructure in windstorms and improved collection of pertinent information from sources, including the design and construction industry, insurance companies, and building officials;

children.label: B
children.text: research, development, and technology transfer to improve loss estimation and risk assessment systems; and

children.label: C
children.text: research, development, and technology transfer to improve simulation and computational modeling of windstorm impacts.

children.label: A
children.text: development of improved outreach and implementation mechanisms to translate existing information and research findings into cost-effective and affordable practices for design and construction professionals, and State and local officials;

children.label: B
children.text: development of cost-effective and affordable windstorm-resistant systems, structures, and materials for use in new construction and retrofit of existing construction; and

children.label: C
children.text: outreach and information dissemination related to cost-effective and affordable construction techniques, loss estimation and risk assessment methodologies, and other pertinent information regarding windstorm phenomena to Federal, State, and local officials, the construction industry, and the general public.

children.heading: Budget activities
children.label: d Budget activities
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology, the Director of the National Science Foundation, the Director of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, and the Director of the Federal Emergency Management Agency shall each include in their agency’s annual budget request to Congress a description of their agency’s projected activities under the Program for the fiscal year covered by the budget request, along with an assessment of what they plan to spend on those activities for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Interagency Coordinating Committee on Windstorm Impact Reduction
children.label: e Interagency Coordinating Committee on Windstorm Impact Reduction

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established an Interagency Coordinating Committee on Windstorm Impact Reduction, chaired by the Director or the Director’s designee.

children.label: i
children.text: the Federal Emergency Management Agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration;

children.label: iii
children.text: the National Science Foundation;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Office of Science and Technology Policy; and

children.label: v
children.text: the Office of Management and Budget; and

children.label: B
children.text: the head of any other Federal agency, or such designee, the chair considers appropriate.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 3 Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall meet not less than once a year at the call of the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

children.heading: General purpose and duties
children.label: 4 General purpose and duties
children.text: The Committee shall oversee the planning and coordination of the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: prioritized goals for the Program that will mitigate against the loss of life and property from future windstorms;

children.label: B
children.text: short-term, mid-term, and long-term research objectives to achieve those goals;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the role of each Program agency in achieving the prioritized goals;

children.label: D
children.text: the methods by which progress towards the goals will be assessed; and

children.label: E
children.text: an explanation of how the Program will foster the transfer of research results into outcomes, such as improved model building codes.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the activities funded under the Program, a description of how these activities align with the prioritized goals and research objectives established in the Strategic Plan, and the budgets, per agency, for these activities;

children.label: B
children.text: the outcomes achieved by the Program for each of the goals identified in the Strategic Plan;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of any recommendations made to change existing building codes that were the result of Program activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the extent to which the Program has incorporated recommendations from the Advisory Committee on Windstorm Impact Reduction.

children.heading: Coordinated budget
children.label: 7 Coordinated budget
children.text: The Committee shall develop a coordinated budget for the Program, which shall be submitted to the Congress not later than 60 days after the date of the President’s budget submission for each fiscal year.
